data.title: Raiffeisen's Deal for Romania Unit Has High Price Tag — Market Talk
data.description: The price Raiffeisen is paying to buy Garanti BBVA Group Romania and to make its Romanian subsidiary the country's third largest bank is high compared with recent deals, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods says in a research note. The 591 million-euro price implies a valuation above book value while Banca Transilvania and Intesa Sanpaolo bought OTP Romania and First Bank respectively below book, the analysts write. It is encouraging to see the Austrian bank deploy excess capital to boost its scale in an existing market, though the Romanian banking sector remains fragmented. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: JD.com's Ceconomy Takeover Faces Regulatory Uncertainty in Austria
data.description:
By Aimee Look

German consumer-electronics retailer Ceconomy said its $2.6 billion acquisition by China's JD.com is facing uncertainty over its foreign investment clearance in Austria.

The Beijing-based e-commerce group made a bid to purchase a slice of Ceconomy, in a push to bolster its international presence. JD.com and partner Convergenta would together hold a 70.9% stake in the retailer, JD.com previously said.

When announced in July, the deal valued Ceconomy at around 2.23 billion euros, or 4.6 euros a share.

Now, the acquisition might be at odds with meeting the required foreign direct investment clearance in Austria, according to Ceconomy.

In Italy and France, FDI clearances have already been obtained, and Ceconomy expects further clearances in Germany and Spain in the near term. But in Austria, it is uncertain whether or when foreign investment clearance will be granted, Ceconomy said.

"Ceconomy and JD.com remain in close dialogue with the Austrian Federal Ministry of Economy, Energy and Tourism in order to satisfy the conditions for obtaining the clearance and to facilitate the successful completion of the Transaction," Ceconomy said.

JD.com and Austria's Ministry of Economy, Energy and Tourism didn't immediately reply to a request for comment.

JD.com is making a push into Europe, up against rising competition from Alibaba and PDD Holdings in China, HSBC analysts said last year. The company has bolstered investment in local-to-local e-commerce in France, the U.K. and Germany, the analysts said.

Write to Aimee Look at aimee.look@wsj.com

data.title: French Retailer Casino Gets Funding Maturity Extension From Creditors
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud

France's Casino Guichard-Perrachon said creditors agreed to extend the maturity of operational funding until May 28, as the French retailer works to sign a deal for a financial restructuring by that date.

The creditors also agreed to extend until May 28 their consent not to use the restructuring talks to take any action regarding the financing documentation, Casino said.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: UK's Bioventix half-year revenue, profit decline amid China market challenges
data.description:
Overview

  • UK monoclonal antibody supplier's half-year revenue declined yr/yr, with pretax profit also falling

  • Tau/neuro royalties for Alzheimer's assays increased five-fold during the reporting period

  • Sales of core antibodies were broadly in line with last year; limited revenue loss in China

Outlook

  • Company says trading remains in line with expectations for year ending 30 June 2026

Result Drivers

  • TAU/NEURO ROYALTIES - Co said royalties from Tau/neuro antibodies for Alzheimer's assays increased five-fold during the period

  • CORE ANTIBODIES - Sales of vitamin D and other core antibodies were broadly in line with last year, reflecting mature diagnostic products

  • CHINA REVENUE LOSS - Co said challenging market conditions in China led to loss of some limited revenue streams

Company press release:

data.title: Skanska Wins $99 Mln Contract To Build High School In Washington
data.description:
SOLNA (dpa-AFX) - Skanska AB (SKA-B.ST, SKSBF, SKBSY), a Swedish construction and development company, on Monday said it has signed a contract with the Issaquah School District to build a new high school in Issaquah, Washington.

The contract is worth $99 million, or about SEK 900 million.

The order will be included in U.S. bookings for the first quarter of 2026.

The construction is scheduled to begin in April 2026 and is expected to be completed in August 2027.

The project includes phase one construction of the new Issaquah High School, comprising a covered parking garage, an athletic field, site amenities, and significant right-of-way work to improve public infrastructure and access.

On Friday, Skanska closed trading 0.69% lesser at SEK 246.10 on the Stockholm Stock Exchange.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: UK's Artisanal Spirits preliminary 2025 revenue falls 16% on US disruption
data.description:
Overview

  • UK premium whisky maker's preliminary 2025 revenue fell 16% yr/yr amid US disruption

  • Co reports adjusted EBITDA loss of £1.9 mln, reflecting US shutdown and RTM change costs

  • Company reported growth in cask sales, venues, and Single Cask Nation, but 25% revenue drop in Asia

Outlook

  • Company says FY26 guidance remains unchanged, with trading in line with expectations

  • Company expects cost savings of about $1 mln over three years from new US route-to-market

  • Company sees early 2026 revenue growth in Asia and America offsetting slower start in Europe

Result Drivers

  • US SHUTDOWN & RTM CHANGE - Co said US government shutdown and route-to-market change prevented anticipated shipments and caused revenue reversal, directly impacting FY25 results

  • ASIA HEADWINDS - Co said continued economic and market headwinds in Asia led to a 25% regional revenue decline

  • COST MANAGEMENT - Co said cost management and efficiencies delivered recurring savings of £0.3 mln in the year, with further savings expected from organisational redesign

data.title: Debenhams sees double-digit profit growth ahead as turnaround gains pace
data.description: British fashion retailer Debenhams said on Monday it expects double-digit percentage growth in adjusted core profit in 2027, raising its outlook as a multi-year turnaround strategy shows signs of taking hold.

data.title: UK's PipeHawk posts H1 net loss on weak demand, rising costs
data.description:
Overview

  • UK utility detection and mapping firm's H1 revenue fell 45% yr/yr amid weak demand

  • Company reported H1 net loss after tax from profit a year earlier

  • Subsidiary Adien declared insolvent, winding-up process underway

Outlook

  • Company expects first global orders for TED via Unipart partnership in the near future

  • Potential Utsi sale, if approved, would provide funding to develop TED's offerings

Result Drivers

  • WEAK CUSTOMER CONFIDENCE - Co said economic and political uncertainty led to customers delaying orders, hurting turnover

  • RISING COSTS - Co said generally increasing costs contributed to lower profitability

  • SUPPLY CHAIN ISSUES - Utsi Electronics faced component shortages and supply chain disruptions, impacting delivery timelines and margins

data.title: France's Equasens FY revenue rises, helped by divisional growth
data.description:
Overview

  • France digital health solutions firm's FY 2025 revenue rose 9.1%, net profit up 8.8%

  • Annual recurring revenue grew 8.8% to €108 mln, reflecting strong demand for digital solutions

  • Company acquired majority stake in Erevo, expanding into digital healthcare training

Outlook

  • Company aims to strengthen leadership in French and European digital health solutions market in 2026

  • Equasens to continue integrating AI into core software and develop new SaaS functionalities in 2026

  • E-CONNECT division faces supply constraints for RAM and components in 2026, has secured supply

Result Drivers

  • INNOVATIVE SOLUTIONS - Co said strong commercial performance in 2025 was driven by its portfolio of digital solutions for healthcare professionals and providers

  • RECURRING REVENUE - CFO said recurring revenue and strong cash generation supported the business model and technology investments

  • DIVISIONAL GROWTH - E-Connect and Medical Solutions divisions posted strong revenue growth, though E-Connect margins fell due to less favourable product mix and higher costs

Company press release:

Key Details

Metric

FY Net Profit

Beat/Miss

Actual

EUR 41.10 mln

Consensus Estimate

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "strong buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy", no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the it services & consulting peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Equasens SA is €52.00, about 52% above its March 27 closing price of €34.20

  • The stock recently traded at 11 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 14 three months ago
